notes: Items in the booth — mostly tapes (recorded fragments) and small physical objects visitors bring or leave. The booth's piles are smaller and more intimate than the diner's. No bindle-construction analogue; the priestess wins by COMPLETING SESSIONS, not by assembling an object.

tape_old_booth_47.title: Tape · BOOTH 47, 11:18 PM
tape_old_booth_47.flavor: A cassette labeled in Elicia's handwriting. The returner's first session.

tape_returner_first_visit.title: Tape · The Returner's First
tape_returner_first_visit.flavor: The first time the returner came in. Eight months ago.

tape_practice_eulogy.title: Tape · Practice Eulogy
tape_practice_eulogy.flavor: A draft someone recorded for someone who hadn't died yet.

water_bottle.title: Water Bottle
water_bottle.flavor: Glass. Heavy. The label is from the cathedral's kitchenette.

tissues.title: Tissues
tissues.flavor: A small travel-pack. Most visitors take one and pocket the rest.

coffee_mug_extra.title: Spare Coffee Mug
coffee_mug_extra.flavor: There are exactly four. Three are clean. One is yours.

old_magazine.title: Old Magazine
old_magazine.flavor: Six months out of date. A page is dog-eared in the back.

xlr_cable_short.title: XLR · Short
xlr_cable_short.flavor: A two-foot cable. Always exactly the right length when you actually need it.
